patches.rationale: CRITICAL: DOI 10.1038/ngeo2516 resolves to Doetterl et al. 2015 (soil carbon), not Stephens et al. Update to Stephens et al. (2012) 'An update on Earth's energy balance in light of the latest global observations' doi:10.1038/ngeo1580.

patches.replace: within the dome's predicted field structure (Swarm/GRACE orbit at 250–500 km, well below the firmament at ~9,086 km)
patches.rationale: Satellites orbit BELOW the dome's firmament, not above it. Correcting this actually strengthens our argument: if the dome's field causes 90% gravity variation, satellites should measure it directly.

patches.find: MT surveys show attenuation valley at 11-12 Hz, not a peak
patches.replace: 11-12 Hz is a Schumann inter-harmonic minimum, not a dome resonance peak; first harmonic at 14.3 Hz disproves any 'cutoff'
patches.rationale: Finding one-liner still says 'attenuation valley' which is inaccurate (implies signal drop, not gap between harmonics). Evidence text was already corrected to 'local power minimum' and 'inter-harmonic minimum' — finding should match.
DOI ad8e69 returns 404. Correct Mistele (2024) ApJL paper DOI is 10.3847/2041-8213/ad54b0.
Angular range should be written low-to-high (104-140°) consistently. The 140-103° notation in one location is reversed.

patches.find: dome is contradicted by data in 1 (Polaris)
patches.replace: dome is contradicted by data in 1 domain (Polaris excess, where the dome model's own refraction formula fails by two orders of magnitude)
patches.rationale: SEC-3.1 bottom line says 'contradicted by data in 1 (Polaris)' but the domain table says 'Neither model is tested by this.' Must reconcile. Since WIN-065 shows the dome's refraction formula predicts 0.04° vs observed 4.5° at Oslo, the data-contradiction framing is justified — update to make this explicit.
